aṇaka gaṇa utkarādi; adj. klein, gering, verachtet  AK. 3,2,4 .  H. 1442 . am Anf. eines comp. als Ausdruck der Geringschätzung  P. 2,1,54 .
— Vgl. aṇiman, aṇu, anaka, āṇaka.

results.rendered_html:
aṇa(na)ka a. [aṇati yathecchaṃ nadati, aṇ-ac kutsāyāṃ kan ca] Very small, contemptible, mean, insignificant, wretched; pāpāṇake kutsitaiḥ  P. II. 1. 54 ; oft. in comp. in the sense of deterioration or contempt; ˚kulālaḥ  Sk.  a contemptible potter.
— kaḥ A kind of bird.
